header.stance: 前端利多推升殖利率曲線陡峭化，長存續期間壓力則一路延續至下午 2:00 提前收盤

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場論點

7 月 2 日美國公債收盤中斷了前2個交易日由實質殖利率主導的拋售，但未帶來廣泛的存續期間行情。較疲弱的美國就業數據帶動政策敏感型殖利率下行，並使聯準會立即升息的情境進一步遠離市場機率分布的中心。長端並未跟進。基準與長債殖利率略高於前一日，使殖利率曲線轉陡，並延續主導 7 月 1 日行情的期限溢價壓力。當日依 SIFMA 建議於美東時間下午 2:00 提前收盤，因此最適合將結果理解為：在長存續期間環境依然嚴峻之際，前端進行政策重新定價，而非一般完整交易時段的最終判斷。

因此，核心解讀在1個重要層面有所改變。7 月 1 日較溫和的通膨訊號與整條殖利率曲線的實質殖利率上升同時出現，意味著成長韌性、Fed 溝通的不確定性及長端出清利率的重要性超過通膨降溫。7 月 2 日削弱了這套論點中的成長面向。6 月非農就業成長大幅放緩，前幾個月數據遭向下修正，勞動參與率下降，工廠訂單也出現萎縮。然而，初領失業救濟金人數仍低，出貨與未完成訂單也持續擴張。這些證據降低了近期升息的急迫性，但尚未顯示勞動市場或需求將迅速斷裂。

3條傳導管道界定了提前收盤的走勢。勞動報告與較低油價改變 Fed 定價，並有利於2年期券種。穩定的損益兩平通膨率與長端實質殖利率正向變動，使基準與長端未能共享這波漲勢。全球存續期間又帶來額外限制：日本政府公債標售疲弱、日圓壓力持續，加上市場擔心 BOJ 可能落後於通膨，使日本殖利率大幅上升；即使 ECB的政策辯論不再那麼急迫，跨市場期限溢價門檻仍然存在。

信用與跨資產表現使整體訊息不像單看利率曲線那麼防禦。上市公司債及新興市場債務指標稍後小幅收高，但投資等級市場日益集中於 AI 相關借款人，在整體定價緊俏之下帶來另一種承接風險。固定收益市場停止交易後，美元走弱、黃金上漲，強化了 Fed 近期政策轉為溫和的解讀。半導體大幅拋售但波動率低迷，顯示風險趨避集中於特定領域，而非系統性事件。因此，本次收盤扭轉了立即升息焦慮，但長存續期間補償、初級市場集中度與全球政策分歧仍未解決。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國財政部於美東時間下午 5:00 記錄的官方每日參考序列顯示，2年期殖利率為 4.14%，下降3個基點；5年期為 4.23%，下降1個基點。10 年期上升1個基點至 4.49%，30 年期則增加1個基點至 4.98%。這些是縮短交易時段的每日參考值，並非 SIFMA 建議美東時間下午 2:00 提前收盤時的精確觀察值。儘管如此，它們仍捕捉到關鍵分化：就業數據公布後，政策敏感型天期上漲，但基準與長存續期間仍維持前一交易日偏高的出清利率。

殖利率曲線斜率清楚反映了這項分歧。2s10s 利差擴大4個基點至 35，2s30s 也擴大4個基點至 84。2s5s10s 蝶式利差維持在-17 不變，顯示5年期相對兩翼並未出現新的錯位。2s10s30s 蝶式利差上升4個基點至-14，因 10 年期相對2年期與長債的平均值走便宜。因此，這波行情是前端由政策主導的牛市趨陡，較長天期殖利率並未同步下降。

從較長觀察期來看，前端上漲尚不足以構成體制轉換。以1週計，2年、5年、10 年及 30 年期殖利率仍分別上升5、8、9及 12 個基點。以1個月計，2年期仍上升9個基點、5年期上升5個基點；相較之下，10 年期僅上升2個基點，30 年期則下降1個基點。7 月 2 日修復了部分立即政策衝擊，但未抹去當週各天期同步走便宜的情況，也未消除前端 Fed 風險與最長端存續期間需求之間長期存在的差異。

6 月就業數據在收盤前構成主要的因果轉折。非農就業人數增加 57,000，低於 113,000的預估中位數，且前2個月數據遭向下修正。失業率降至 4.2%，但這項下降反映勞動參與率大幅下滑，限制了其鷹派意涵。短期利率期貨將 7 月 29 日升息的機率，由數據公布前約 33% 下調至約 20%。市場仍完全定價年內升息一碼，但預期時點由 10 月延後至 12 月。

同時公布的數據使重新定價保持克制。截至 6 月 27 日當週，初領失業救濟金人數減少 1,000 至 215,000，4週平均值降至 222,000。續領失業救濟金人數增加 2,000 至 181.4萬，4週平均值則升至 180.3萬。新增裁員仍受控制，但續領人數顯示再就業速度放緩。5 月工廠訂單在 4 月增加 5.3% 後下降 1.3%，但出貨增加 1.6%、未完成訂單成長 0.6%、庫存增加 0.2%。需求降溫，但生產流程並未崩潰。

通膨連結定價說明了較長天期名目殖利率為何未受勞動市場利多帶動。10 年期實質殖利率上升1個基點至 2.26%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率則維持在 2.23% 不變。5年期實質殖利率增加1個基點至 1.99%；7年期維持在 2.11%；20 年及 30 年期實質殖利率分別上升2個與1個基點，至 2.60% 與 2.79%。基準名目殖利率上升源於實質補償，而非通膨預期再度升高。油價走低有助於抑制損益兩平通膨率，但並未降低中長存續期間所要求的實質報酬。

現券國庫券的走勢比附息債殖利率曲線更不一致。4週期票息等值殖利率上升4個基點至 3.67%，8週期增加3個基點至 3.72%。6週期殖利率下降3個基點至 3.67%；13、17、26 及 52 週期殖利率則下降3至4個基點，收於 3.76% 至 3.95% 之間。壓力集中於縮短交易時段內出售的天期，並不像全面性的假期融資緊縮。

28 天期標售以 3.605%的最高得標利率接受 $850.001367億，投標倍數為 2.72。間接投標人取得競爭性得標的 67.05%，直接投標人為 3.75%，交易商為 29.20%；最高得標利率的分配比例為 46.77%。56 天期標售以 3.650% 接受 $850.007166億，投標倍數為 2.70。其間接、直接及交易商比例分別為 61.72%、3.90% 及 34.38%，最高得標利率的分配比例為 53.99%。2檔證券皆於 7 月 7 日發行與交割，因此投標結果確立了假期前的承接能力，但現金移轉仍是稍後才會發生的事件。

因此，提前收盤將本週稍早同步變動的2個問題區分開來。非農就業與勞動參與率降低了 Fed 立即採取行動的必要性，而數據公布前的短部位很可能放大了前端漲勢。長天期仍面對正實質殖利率、即將到來的附息債供給，以及尚未穩定的全球期限溢價。美東時間下午 5:00 記錄的股票、貨幣、商品與波動率收盤值，只能作為稍後證據來確認或增加這項解讀的複雜性；它們並未在下午 2:00 截止後造成已完成的固定收益走勢。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

利率市場收盤後，整體上市信用資產基調展現韌性。以美東時間下午 5:00的股票市場參考值計，LQD 上漲 0.1660%，HYG 上漲 0.1508%，JNK 增加 0.1147%。儘管半導體受到衝擊，各信用品質層級方向溫和且相近，並未顯示廣泛的風險清算。更重要的信用訊號來自投資等級債供給結構的改變，以及承接這些供給所需的能力，而非上市基金的大幅波動。

高信評科技公司發債在機械性上改善了指數品質，卻也提高集中度。截至 6 月底，AA- 與 A 評級債券占美國投資等級指數的 52%，高於 2021 年約 46%；BBB 曝險則由 46% 降至 41%。7 月 1 日投資等級利差僅為 0.74 個百分點，接近數十年低點。因此，強勁的現行信評支撐整體定價緊俏，卻也使指數更加依賴少數現金充裕的科技業借款人。

供給規模進一步加深了這項依賴。2026 年以來，企業已在各幣別市場出售約 $2,200億的 AI 相關債券，較 2025 全年多出 62%。Amazon、Meta、Alphabet 與 Oracle 4家超大規模業者，合計約占 $1,070億的美元發債。Nvidia 與 SpaceX 在 6 月各自發行 $250億，協助單月投資等級債發行量創下紀錄。美國六大超大規模業者正準備於 2026 年投入近 $8,200億的 AI 支出，顯示初級市場承接仍是反覆出現的需求，而非已完成的融資事件。

資產負債表強勁可降低立即違約風險，但無法消除到期或投資報酬風險。較長天期債券的存續時間可能超過其融資資產的經濟效用：Alphabet 發行了 100 年期英鎊債務，Nvidia 與 SpaceX 則納入 20 年及 30 年期券次。SpaceX 第1次發行的債券很快在次級市場走弱，顯示信評與表面需求並不保證定價後表現穩定。即使基本現金流維持健全，持續發債仍可能因供給而推升利差；AI 報酬若不如預期，則會額外帶來槓桿與再融資壓力。

日本呈現另一種初級市場調整形式。企業上半年發行 ¥1兆的可轉換公司債，為 2004 年以來同期最高。Nippon Steel的 ¥6,000億交易是日本史上規模最大的可轉換公司債發行，JX Advanced Metals 則籌得 ¥2,500億。許多交易採零息設計，使發行人可降低現金利息成本，同時由較高的股票估值提供轉換價值。國內政策利率上升，使這種混合型管道更具吸引力，但其經濟效益同時取決於股票表現與債權人保障。

假期前的國庫券標售顯示，現金投資人能在交易中斷前承接大量政府供給，但 7 月 7 日交割仍留下稍後的融資檢驗。聯準會於美東時間下午 4:30 公布的 H.4.1 數據只能提供後續背景：截至 7 月 1 日當週，準備金餘額平均為 $2.967兆，增加 $155億，同期美國財政部 General Account 減少 $385億。這份稍後公布的流動性概況無法解釋較早的收盤走勢。它確實顯示，在下一個交割週期到來前，準備金已經增加，並伴隨附買回交易上升及證券投資組合縮小。

因此，公開發債、可轉換公司債與上市風險工具的信用管道仍然暢通，但投資人所融資的標的呈現實質差異。強勁的現行信評及發行人大量現金流支撐承接；緊俏利差、資產年限長及反覆出現的 AI 供給，則縮小了失望的容忍空間。關鍵斷層是集中度與結構，而非當日融資管道關閉。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐洲數據延續了未能在 7 月 1 日帶動美國公債上漲的通膨降溫動能。歐元區 6 月通膨率由 5 月的 3.2% 放緩至 2.8%，低於 3.0%的預估。5 月失業率維持在 6.2%，失業人數較 4 月減少 55,000，較1年前減少 158,000。第1季房價仍較1年前上漲 4.7%，但增幅已由第4季的 5.1% 放緩。這組數據降低了即時價格壓力，卻未顯示勞動市場或家庭資產負債表疲弱。

ECB 溝通仍保留政策的雙向結果。Christine Lagarde 為 6 月升息辯護，因為作出決定時，能源衝擊正在經濟中擴散。她也承認，第2輪效應並未出現。Philip Lane 仍關注通膨延後傳導至薪資、食品與服務的風險；Olli Rehn 與 Martin Kocher 則認為，油價回到戰前水準附近後，近期威脅已經減弱。因此，歐洲前端利率面臨的急迫性下降，但 Governing Council 尚未宣告通膨衝擊已經解決。

英國則帶來更明確的政策上行尾端風險。英格蘭銀行決策官員 Catherine Mann 支持目前將政策利率維持在 3.75%，但她表示，若通膨預期、能源成本、利潤率行為或不利的薪資談判持續，可能需要主動升息。她的立場是在上次會議以 7-2 票反對升息一碼之後提出。若預期發生漂移，對全球存續期間的直接傳導便是英鎊利率的條件式底部上升；即使目前油價壓力緩解，降低了立即行動的必要性。

日本則明確朝相反方向發展。基準 10 年期 JGB 標售的投標倍數為 3.13，低於前次標售的 3.53，也低於 3.33的1年平均值。10 年期殖利率上升7個基點至 2.77%，20 年期則上升 9.5 個基點至 3.76%。投資人衡量財政擴張，以及 BOJ 漸進緊縮可能讓通膨壓力持續的風險，導致需求轉弱。這次標售使長端疑慮透過投標與次級市場殖利率變得可觀察，而不僅存在於政策評論中。

貨幣、政策及買方基礎的證據進一步強化這項壓力。BOJ 已將基準利率調升至 1%，為 1995 年以來最高；但由於日圓仍然疲弱，市場認為 10 月前再次升息的機率超過 60%。企業在 Tankan 調查中的較長期通膨預期升至 2.6%，為 2014 年序列開始以來最高。海外投資人 6 月賣出 ¥3.12兆的日本債券，為3年來最大流出；同時 BOJ 購債減少、政府供給增加。這些管道提高了留住買方所需的國內殖利率。

財政預期又增加了另一層溢價。總理 Sanae Takaichi的目標，是在 2040 年 4 月開始的會計年度，將名目 GDP 擴張至 $6.8兆；投資人並等待可能暫停對食品與飲料課徵消費稅的細節。這項政策可能支撐名目經濟活動，但在央行謹慎正常化之際，也引發發債與通膨方面的疑問。較高的 JGB 出清利率可能降低日本投資人持有外國存續期間資產的相對吸引力，即使沒有同步的通膨衝擊，也會將壓力傳導至其他主權債長端。

澳洲則提供較小的區域成長制衡力量。5 月商品貿易餘額由 A$13.83億順差轉為 A$30.18億逆差，出口下降 6.9%，進口上升 2.6%。非貨幣性黃金出口下滑 35%，造成大部分出口疲弱。數據於 7 月 1 日美東時間晚上 9:30 公布，早於美國縮短交易時段，指向較疲弱的外部需求動能。這項訊號可能抑制澳洲存續期間資產的殖利率，但範圍過於有限，不足以抵銷更強的日本期限溢價管道。

因此，全球存續期間的分歧擴大，而非一致轉為寬鬆。歐元區通膨降溫降低了迅速追加緊縮的機率，澳洲貿易也轉弱。日本則同時面臨標售需求疲弱、外資賣超、財政企圖與貨幣相關通膨風險，英格蘭銀行也保留條件式升息威脅。美國長端在非農就業疲弱後仍拒絕上漲，符合這項國際背景：當主權債供給與買方誘因朝不同方向發展時，近期政策急迫性下降，可與偏高的實質殖利率及期限溢價門檻同時存在。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

美國勞動數據公布後，新興市場金融條件略有改善。隨投資人降低對 Fed 緊縮急迫性的評估，主要新興市場貨幣上漲，其中南非蘭特與匈牙利福林升值約 1%，數個拉丁美洲貨幣也走高。Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index 在美國交易時段下跌 0.5%。傳導機制在貨幣上最為明顯：Fed 不再急於升息，降低了相對利率門檻，也減輕美元負債的償付成本。

在美國重新定價之前，本幣主權債需求已展現韌性。6 月流入泰國、印尼、印度與馬來西亞政府債券的外資達 $82億，為逾2年來最強的合計金額。即使投資人擔心 Fed 再度緊縮，市場預期區域央行將維持高利率，仍支撐了利差收益。自美國與伊朗達成協議以來，1項亞洲新興市場債券指數上漲 0.2%，拉丁美洲指標則下跌 0.6%，顯示能源曝險與國內政策仍造成區域分歧。

同樣的韌性也包含限制。偏高的本地利率可吸引外資，但也反映通膨持續及國內融資條件較為緊縮。南韓 6 月消費者通膨率由 3.1% 加速至 3.2%，核心通膨則維持在 2.5%。強勁出口、貨幣疲弱與房市壓力，使南韓央行維持緊縮傾向。對主權殖利率曲線而言，只有當國內政策可信度與貨幣穩定足以補償投資人的利率風險時，外資流入才能支撐承接。

較晚收盤的美元債提供溫和確認，EMB 以美東時間下午 5:00的參考值計上漲 0.1875%。這項總報酬同時包含美國公債存續期間與主權風險，因此國家層級的融資條件仍比整體走勢更具資訊含量。美元走弱有利於硬通貨借款人與商品進口國，但並未解決短期到期、外匯存底薄弱或依賴外資流入之發行人的再融資風險。

土耳其與哥倫比亞仍是這類脆弱性的例子，因2國更依賴外部資本；若 Fed 重返鷹派路徑，將面臨更劇烈的緊縮。油價月度大幅下跌有助於改善進口國的貿易與通膨計算，能源收入減少則不利於出口國。7 月 2 日減輕了共同的美元流動性壓力，但並未產生一致的主權債結果。本幣債、硬通貨債券與企業融資，仍各自對政策可信度、準備能力及貿易條件的不同組合做出反應。

## 跨資產

以美東時間下午 5:00 紐約參考值記錄的跨資產表現，大致強化了固定收益市場收盤後的前端政策重新定價。科技股拋售在下午 2:00 前已經展開，但股票、波動率、貨幣與商品的最終數值都是較晚的證據。它們可以顯示利率市場的解讀是否延續至當日其餘時段，卻不能顛倒縮短美國公債交易時段的因果順序。

股票表現揭示了明顯的內部分歧。S&P 500 幾乎持平於 7,483.24，Nasdaq 100 下跌 1.61%，費城半導體指數則重挫 5.4448%。儘管出現這些跌幅，VIX 仍下降 2.6522% 至 16.15。大盤穩定且隱含波動率下降，不支持系統性成長衝擊的解讀；晶片股重挫則動搖了市場對該產業承擔大部分投資等級借款與資本支出的信心。

美元與黃金更直接呼應 Fed 近期壓力下降。美元指數下跌 0.5227% 至 100.86，黃金上漲 1.0914% 至 $4,112.70。美元走弱改善全球融資條件，並與立即升息機率下降一致。黃金上漲也反映殖利率下降及無息資產需求回升；但在大盤股票持平、波動率下降之際，並不代表全面性的避險事件。

能源維持中期通膨降溫背景，但未帶來新的單日動能。WTI 收於 $68.69，上漲 0.1604%；Brent 收於 $71.80，上漲 0.3214%。以1個月計，兩者分別下跌 25.4666% 與 24.4051%。近乎持平的單日收盤，說明 10 年期損益兩平通膨率為何在近期回落後保持穩定。規模大得多的月度跌幅，仍是 Fed 與 ECB 官員所提通膨焦慮下降的核心因素。

綜合而言，稍後市場證實了對美國公債走勢較為狹義的解讀。美元走弱與黃金上漲支持 Fed 緊縮不再那麼急迫；大盤股票持平且波動率下降，否定了將就業數據不如預期解讀為經濟衰退。半導體跌勢使成長敘事更加複雜，也凸顯信用集中度，但未帶來廣泛的避險行情。尚待解決的跨資產問題是：AI 修正會維持在特定產業，還是開始削弱投資、發債及風險偏好，進而足以帶動長端實質殖利率下行。

## 前瞻市場意義

假期後首波美國總體經濟數據將於 7 月 6 日美東時間上午 10:00 展開，包括 ISM 服務業指數與紐約聯準銀行全球供應鏈壓力指數；接著是 7 月 7 日上午 8:30的 5 月貿易數據，以及上午 11:00的 6 月消費者預期調查。6 月 16-17 日 FOMC 會議紀要則於 7 月 8 日下午 2:00 公布。服務業就業與價格組合若轉弱、物流成本受控，且家庭通膨預期下降，將證實非農就業放緩足以使 7 月政策維持不變，反應最強的將是2至5年期殖利率。服務業需求若展現韌性、價格壓力再起，或會議紀要顯示廣泛支持於 2026 年升息，將挑戰前端利多，並恢復導致前一週拋售的限制性反應函數。

7 月 6 日至 7 月 9 日，美國財政部供給承接將成為另一項獨立檢驗。7 月 6 日標售包括美東時間上午 11:30的 $920億 13 週期國庫券及 $790億 26 週期國庫券；7 月 7 日則有 $900億 42 天期國庫券、$520億 52 週期國庫券及 $580億 3年期公債。7 月 8 日續發 $390億 10 年期公債，7 月 9 日續發 $220億 30 年期公債，完成本輪序列。投標廣泛、交易商得標比例受控，加上 7 月 9 日國庫券及 7 月 15 日附息債交割有序，將化解眼前的假期融資疑慮，同時支持目前的殖利率曲線分化。參與疲弱或次級市場持續走便宜，則會重新引發資產負債表疑慮，並從現券國庫券傳導至前端流動性，或從附息債供給傳導至長端期限溢價。

全球存續期間分歧將在日本與歐元區面臨最明確的檢驗。日本約 ¥3.8兆的3個月期國庫券標售將於 7 月 3 日舉行，約 ¥6,000億的 30 年期 JGB 標售於 7 月 7 日舉行，約 ¥2.5兆的5年期標售則於 7 月 9 日舉行；歐元區生產者價格及零售銷售將於 7 月 6 日美東時間上午 5:00 公布。若日本終端投資人需求強勁，且歐洲生產流程價格與消費轉弱，將挑戰 7 月 2 日全球期限溢價限制的持續性，並讓美國長端殖利率更充分反映勞動市場轉弱。JGB 標售若再度疲弱、日圓壓力再起，或歐元區價格堅挺，將證實政策與買方基礎的分歧，使長端即使在 Fed 維持利率不變時，仍容易受到實質殖利率與供給補償的影響。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 美國固定收益資料列為 SIFMA 建議於美東時間下午 2:00 結束之交易時段的同日官方每日參考值；其來源時間戳記均予保留。收盤較晚的跨資產資料列，僅在有同日數據時呈現。

overall_summary.headline: 疲弱就業數據提振前端美國公債，但實質殖利率壓力仍牽制長端
overall_summary.session_narrative: 截至 SIFMA 建議的美東時間下午 2:00 收盤，較弱的非農就業成長、向下修正及較低的勞動參與率，已使 Fed 定價轉向較晚升息，帶動前端殖利率下行。穩定的損益兩平通膨率、長端實質殖利率正向變動，以及日本存續期間需求疲弱，限制了5年期以外天期的漲勢。稍後美元走弱、黃金上漲及上市信用資產展現韌性，證實市場較偏向有限度的政策舒緩解讀；半導體遭拋售則仍集中於產業層面，而非系統性事件。
overall_summary.summary: 較疲弱的就業數據降低了 Fed 近期升息的急迫性，並支撐政策敏感型美國公債。較長天期則未能跟進，因實質殖利率、供給需求與全球期限溢價壓力依然穩固，使提前收盤時的殖利率曲線轉陡，而非出現廣泛的存續期間行情。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤消除了立即發生政策衝擊的風險，但長端的出清門檻依然存在。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察假期過後，較疲弱的成長證據是否終於能帶動長端實質殖利率下行。
section_summaries.summary: 7 月 2 日較疲弱的勞動數據與較低油價扭轉了立即升息的焦慮，形成由前端主導的殖利率曲線陡峭化，而非廣泛的存續期間行情。穩定的通膨補償、偏高的實質殖利率、日本期限溢價壓力，以及集中於 AI的融資需求，使較長天期與信用風險仍未化解。

section_summaries.market_read: 前端的 Fed 升息急迫性下降，但實質殖利率需求維持了殖利率曲線偏陡的傾向。
section_summaries.next_check: 服務業價格與就業將顯示，前端利多能否延續至非農就業數據以外。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 非農就業數據令人失望，加上勞動參與率轉弱，使 Fed 定價轉向較晚升息，推動政策主導的牛市趨陡，但長端實質補償並未下降。裁員仍受控制、生產流程維持完整，加上單週殖利率變動仍為正值，使行情幅度保持克制，尚不足以構成體制轉換。

section_summaries.market_read: 融資管道保持暢通，但同時存在整體定價未充分反映的集中度溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 隨 AI 相關發債延續，追蹤次級市場表現與交易商承接情況。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 上市債務指標稍後顯示整體風險基調具韌性，但投資等級債券的緊俏定價，掩蓋了市場日益依賴少數 AI 借款人的情況。強勁的信評與現金流支撐市場准入，但反覆出現的長天期供給及不確定的專案報酬，縮小了發行後表現不如預期的容忍空間。

section_summaries.market_read: 當買方需求與供給分歧時，全球寬鬆訊號不再保證長端殖利率下降。
section_summaries.next_check: 日本接下來的標售將檢驗終端需求疲弱是否會持續對主權債期限溢價構成壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區通膨降溫降低了近期緊縮的急迫性，但日本標售需求疲弱、外資賣超、財政企圖與日圓相關通膨壓力，提高了全球存續期間的門檻。結果是政策分歧：歐洲條件轉為寬鬆，可與美國及日本長債偏高的出清利率同時存在。

section_summaries.market_read: 美元壓力緩解使新興市場流動性略有改善，但承接能力仍取決於各國內部政策可信度。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察 Fed 壓力緩解能否持續，而不致因貨幣走弱迫使本地政策維持緊縮。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元走弱及 Fed 近期升息急迫性下降，降低了新興市場共同面對的融資門檻，並支撐貨幣及稍後上市債務資產的表現。然而，限制性的本地政策、能源曝險不一及對外資流入的依賴，仍維持國家層級的分歧，並使脆弱借款人容易受到鷹派逆轉衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 稍後跨資產表現證實政策壓力緩解，但對 AI 主導的成長與融資敘事提出挑戰。
section_summaries.next_check: 關鍵檢驗在於半導體疲弱是否會擴散至投資、發債或整體風險資產。
section_summaries.summary: 固定收益市場收盤後，美元走弱與黃金上漲支撐升息機率下降的解讀；大盤股票持平且波動率下降，則否定了系統性成長衝擊。半導體重挫反而使疑慮集中於 AI 投資、發債及長端實質殖利率壓力的持久性。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線若要全面上漲，既需要較溫和的政策訊號，也需要可信的長存續期間承接能力。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注 7 月 6-9 日的數據與標售序列，觀察前端與長端的分歧是否出現突破。
section_summaries.summary: 3項檢驗將決定前端利多能否擴大：假期後的美國總體經濟數據序列、財政部的國庫券與附息債供給，以及日本標售與歐元區數據。經濟活動轉弱且投標秩序良好，將驗證目前的分化；價格壓力再起或存續期間需求疲弱，則會恢復緊縮與期限溢價風險。

watch_events.market_implication: 疲弱將延續2至5年期殖利率的利多；韌性則會恢復升息風險。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 2 Treasury close interrupted the real-yield-led selloff of the previous 2 sessions without delivering a broad duration rally. Softer US employment data pulled policy-sensitive yields lower and pushed an immediate Federal Reserve increase further from the center of the market’s distribution. The long end did not follow. Benchmark and long-bond yields held slightly above the prior day, leaving the curve steeper and preserving the term-premium pressure that had dominated July 1. This was a SIFMA-recommended early close at 2:00 p.m. ET, so the outcome is best understood as a front-end policy repricing inside a still-demanding long-duration environment, not as a regular full-session verdict.

The central interpretation therefore changed in one important respect. July 1 had combined softer inflation signals with rising real yields across the curve, implying that growth resilience, uncertain Fed communication and the long-end clearing rate outweighed disinflation. July 2 weakened the growth side of that argument. June payroll growth slowed sharply, earlier months were revised down, participation fell and factory orders contracted. Initial claims remained low, however, and shipments and backlogs still expanded. The evidence reduced the urgency of a near-term hike without establishing a rapid labor or demand break.

3 transmission channels defined the shortened close. The labor report and lower oil shifted Fed pricing and favored the 2-year sector. Stable breakevens and positive long real-yield changes kept the benchmark and long end from sharing that rally. Global duration supplied an additional restraint: a weak Japanese government-bond auction, persistent yen pressure and concern that the BOJ could fall behind inflation pushed Japanese yields sharply higher, sustaining a cross-market term-premium hurdle even as the ECB debate became less urgent.

Credit and cross assets made the message less defensive than the rates curve alone might suggest. Listed corporate and emerging-market debt gauges later finished modestly higher, yet the high-grade market’s growing concentration in AI-related borrowers raised a separate absorption risk beneath tight aggregate pricing. The dollar weakened and gold rose after the fixed-income cutoff, reinforcing the softer near-term Fed interpretation. A deep semiconductor selloff alongside subdued volatility showed that risk aversion was concentrated, not systemic. The close thus reversed immediate hike anxiety while leaving long-duration compensation, primary-market concentration and global policy dispersion unresolved.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury’s official daily reference series, timestamped 5:00 p.m. ET, recorded the 2-year yield at 4.14%, down 3 basis points, and the 5-year at 4.23%, down 1. The 10-year rose 1 basis point to 4.49%, and the 30-year added 1 to 4.98%. These are daily references for the shortened session, not exact observations at the SIFMA-recommended early close at 2:00 p.m. ET. They nevertheless capture the defining split: policy-sensitive maturities rallied after the employment release, while benchmark and long duration retained the prior session’s elevated clearing rate.

Curve slopes translated that divergence cleanly. The 2s10s spread widened 4 basis points to 35, and 2s30s widened 4 to 84. The 2s5s10s butterfly was unchanged at -17, showing no fresh dislocation in the 5-year against its wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly rose 4 basis points to -14 as the 10-year cheapened relative to the average of the 2-year and long bond. The move was therefore a policy-led bull steepening at the front, with no corresponding fall in longer yields.

The broader horizon prevented the front-end rally from becoming a regime change. Over 1 week, 2-, 5-, 10- and 30-year yields were still higher by 5, 8, 9 and 12 basis points. Over 1 month, the 2-year remained 9 basis points higher and the 5-year 5 higher, whereas the 10-year was only 2 higher and the 30-year 1 lower. July 2 repaired part of the immediate policy shock but did not erase the week’s synchronized cheapening or the longer-running difference between front-end Fed risk and far-end duration demand.

June employment supplied the main causal break before the cutoff. Nonfarm payrolls increased by 57,000, below the 113,000 median estimate, after downward revisions to the previous 2 months. Unemployment fell to 4.2%, but the decline reflected a sharp drop in labor-force participation, limiting its hawkish content. Short-rate futures reduced the probability of a July 29 increase to about 20% from roughly 33% before the release. A quarter-point move remained fully priced for the year, though the expected timing shifted to December from October.

The accompanying releases kept the repricing measured. Initial claims fell by 1,000 to 215,000 in the week ended June 27, and their 4-week average declined to 222,000. Insured unemployment rose by 2,000 to 1.814 million, with its 4-week average increasing to 1.803 million. The flow of new layoffs remained contained even as continued claims suggested slower re-employment. May factory orders fell 1.3% after April’s 5.3% increase, yet shipments rose 1.6%, unfilled orders gained 0.6% and inventories increased 0.2%. Demand cooled, but production pipelines did not collapse.

Inflation-linked pricing explains why longer nominal yields resisted the labor relief. The 10-year real yield rose 1 basis point to 2.26%, while the 10-year breakeven was unchanged at 2.23%. 5-year real yields added 1 basis point to 1.99%; 7-year real yields held at 2.11%; and 20- and 30-year real yields rose 2 and 1 basis points to 2.60% and 2.79%. The benchmark nominal increase came from real compensation, not a renewed rise in inflation expectations. Cheaper oil helped restrain breakevens, but it did not reduce the real return required for intermediate and long duration.

Cash bills were more uneven than the coupon curve. 4-week coupon-equivalent yields rose 4 basis points to 3.67%, and 8-week yields added 3 to 3.72%. 6-week yields fell 3 basis points to 3.67%, while 13-, 17-, 26- and 52-week yields declined by 3 to 4 basis points, ending between 3.76% and 3.95%. The pattern concentrated pressure in the maturities sold during the shortened session and did not resemble a uniform holiday funding squeeze.

The 28-day auction accepted $85.0001367 billion at a 3.605% high rate with 2.72 bid-to-cover. Indirect bidders took 67.05% of competitive awards, direct bidders 3.75% and dealers 29.20%; 46.77% was allotted at the high. The 56-day sale accepted $85.0007166 billion at 3.650% with 2.70 coverage. Its indirect, direct and dealer shares were 61.72%, 3.90% and 34.38%, and 53.99% was allotted at the high. Both securities issue and settle July 7, so the bidding outcome established pre-holiday absorption while the cash transfer remains a later event.

The shortened close consequently separated 2 questions that had moved together earlier in the week. Payrolls and participation reduced the need for an imminent Fed response, and pre-release short positioning likely amplified the front-end rally. Long maturities still faced positive real yields, upcoming coupon supply and an unsettled global term premium. Equity, currency, commodity and volatility closes recorded at 5:00 p.m. ET can confirm or complicate that interpretation only as later evidence; they did not cause the completed fixed-income move after the 2:00 p.m. ET cutoff.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Broad listed credit tone was resilient after the rates close. At the 5:00 p.m. ET equity-market reference, LQD gained 0.1660%, HYG rose 0.1508% and JNK added 0.1147%. The modest, similar direction across quality tiers did not indicate a broad risk liquidation despite the semiconductor shock. The more consequential credit signal came from the changing composition of high-grade supply and the capacity required to absorb it, not from a large move in listed funds.

High-rated technology issuance has mechanically improved index quality while increasing concentration. AA- and A-rated bonds represented 52% of the US investment-grade index at the end of June, up from about 46% in 2021, as BBB exposure fell to 41% from 46%. High-grade spreads were only 0.74 percentage point on July 1, near multi-decade lows. Strong current ratings therefore supported tight aggregate pricing, but they also made the index more dependent on a narrow group of cash-rich technology borrowers.

The supply scale sharpened that dependence. Companies had sold about $220 billion of AI-linked bonds across currencies during 2026, 62% more than in all of 2025. Four hyperscalers—Amazon, Meta, Alphabet and Oracle—accounted for roughly $107 billion of dollar issuance. Nvidia and SpaceX each sold $25 billion in June, helping monthly high-grade volume reach a record. The six largest US hyperscalers were preparing nearly $820 billion of AI spending during 2026, suggesting that primary-market absorption remains a recurring requirement rather than a completed funding event.

Balance-sheet strength lowers immediate default risk but does not remove maturity or return-on-investment risk. Longer bonds can outlive the economic usefulness of the assets they finance: Alphabet issued sterling debt with a 100-year maturity, and Nvidia and SpaceX included 20- and 30-year tranches. SpaceX’s debut bonds weakened rapidly in secondary trading, showing that ratings and headline demand do not guarantee stable post-pricing performance. Continued issuance could widen spreads through supply even if underlying cash flows remain sound; disappointing AI returns would add leverage and refinancing pressure.

Japan offered a different form of primary-market adaptation. Companies sold ¥1 trillion of convertible bonds in H1, the most for that period since 2004. Nippon Steel’s ¥600 billion transaction was the largest Japanese convertible issue on record, and JX Advanced Metals raised ¥250 billion. Many deals carried zero coupons, allowing issuers to reduce cash interest costs while higher equity valuations supplied conversion value. Rising domestic policy rates made that hybrid channel more attractive, though its economics depend on equity performance as well as creditor protection.

Pre-holiday bill auctions showed that cash investors could absorb substantial government supply before the interruption, but their July 7 settlement leaves a later funding test. The Federal Reserve’s H.4.1 release at 4:30 p.m. ET supplied subsequent context only: reserve balances averaged $2.967 trillion in the week ended July 1, up $15.5 billion, as the Treasury General Account fell $38.5 billion. That later liquidity snapshot cannot explain the earlier close. It does show that reserves had risen going into the next settlement cycle, alongside higher reverse repos and a smaller securities portfolio.

Credit access thus remained open across public bonds, convertibles and listed risk vehicles, with material differentiation in what investors were financing. High current ratings and large issuer cash flows supported absorption; tight spreads, long asset lives and repeated AI supply reduced the margin for disappointment. The key fault line was concentration and structure, not a same-day shutdown in funding.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European data extended the disinflation impulse that had failed to rally Treasuries on July 1. Euro-area inflation slowed to 2.8% in June from 3.2% in May and came in below a 3.0% estimate. May unemployment held at 6.2%, with the number of unemployed people down 55,000 from April and 158,000 from a year earlier. Q1 house prices still rose 4.7% from a year earlier, although that pace eased from 5.1% in Q4. The combination reduced immediate price pressure without presenting a weak labor or household-balance-sheet backdrop.

ECB communication preserved a two-sided policy consequence. Christine Lagarde defended June’s increase because the energy shock had been spreading through the economy when the decision was made. She also acknowledged that second-round effects had not materialized. Philip Lane remained attentive to delayed pass-through into wages, food and services, whereas Olli Rehn and Martin Kocher judged the near-term threat to have diminished after oil returned toward prewar levels. European front-end rates therefore faced less urgency, but the Governing Council had not declared the inflation shock resolved.

The UK supplied a more explicit upside-policy tail. Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann supported holding the policy rate at 3.75% for now, yet said persistent inflation expectations, energy costs, profit-margin behavior or adverse wage negotiations could warrant an activist increase. Her stance followed a 7-2 vote against a quarter-point hike at the prior meeting. The direct transmission to global duration is a higher conditional floor for sterling rates if expectations drift, even as current oil relief reduces the need for immediate action.

Japan moved decisively in the other direction. A benchmark 10-year JGB auction produced a 3.13 bid-to-cover ratio, down from 3.53 at the previous sale and below the 3.33 1-year average. The 10-year yield rose 7 basis points to 2.77%, and the 20-year climbed 9.5 basis points to 3.76%. Demand weakened as investors weighed fiscal expansion and the risk that gradual BOJ tightening would allow inflation pressure to persist. The auction made the long-end concern observable through bidding and secondary yields, not merely through policy commentary.

Currency, policy and buyer-base evidence reinforced that pressure. The BOJ had raised its benchmark rate to 1%, the highest since 1995, yet markets assigned more than a 60% probability to another increase by October as the yen remained weak. Firms’ longer-term inflation expectations in the Tankan reached 2.6%, the highest in the series beginning in 2014. Overseas investors sold ¥3.12 trillion of Japanese bonds during June, the largest outflow in 3 years, as BOJ purchases receded and government supply rose. Those channels increase the domestic yield needed to retain buyers.

Fiscal expectations added a separate premium. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi targeted an expansion of nominal GDP to $6.8 trillion in the fiscal year beginning April 2040, and investors awaited details on a possible suspension of the sales tax on food and beverages. The policy may support nominal activity, but it also raises questions about issuance and inflation while the central bank normalizes cautiously. A higher JGB clearing rate can reduce the relative appeal of foreign duration for Japanese investors, transmitting pressure into other sovereign long ends even without a synchronized inflation shock.

Australia contributed a smaller regional growth counterweight. Its May goods balance swung to an A$3.018 billion deficit from a A$1.383 billion surplus as exports fell 6.9% and imports rose 2.6%. A 35% decline in non-monetary-gold exports drove most of the export weakness. The release occurred at 9:30 p.m. ET on July 1, before the US shortened session, and pointed to a softer external-demand impulse. That signal could restrain Australian duration, but it was too narrow to offset the stronger Japanese term-premium channel.

Global duration therefore became more dispersed, not uniformly easier. Euro-area disinflation reduced the probability of rapid follow-up tightening, and Australian trade softened. Japan combined weak auction demand, foreign selling, fiscal ambition and currency-linked inflation risk, while the BOE kept a conditional hike threat alive. The US long end’s refusal to rally after soft payrolls fits that international backdrop: lower near-term policy urgency can coexist with a high real and term-premium hurdle when sovereign supply and buyer incentives are moving in different directions.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market financial conditions improved at the margin after the US labor release. Major EM currencies rose as investors reduced the urgency of Fed tightening, with the South African rand and Hungarian forint gaining about 1% and several Latin American currencies advancing. The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index was down 0.5% during the US session. The mechanism was clearest in currencies: a less immediate Fed hike lowers the relative-rate hurdle and eases the cost of servicing dollar liabilities.

Local-currency sovereign demand had already shown resilience before the US repricing. Foreign inflows into government bonds in Thailand, Indonesia, India and Malaysia reached $8.2 billion in June, the strongest combined total in more than 2 years. Expectations that regional central banks would keep rates elevated supported carry even when investors feared renewed Fed tightening. Since the US-Iran agreement, an emerging-Asia bond index had gained 0.2%, compared with a 0.6% decline in a Latin American gauge, demonstrating that energy exposure and domestic policy still produced regional dispersion.

The same resilience contains a constraint. Elevated local rates can attract foreign capital, but they also reflect persistent inflation and tighter domestic financing conditions. South Korean consumer inflation accelerated to 3.2% in June from 3.1%, while core inflation held at 2.5%. Strong exports, a weak currency and housing pressure kept the Bank of Korea’s bias restrictive. For sovereign curves, foreign inflows can support absorption only while domestic credibility and currency stability compensate investors for the rate risk.

Later-closing dollar debt provided mild confirmation, with EMB gaining 0.1875% at the 5:00 p.m. ET reference. That total return combined Treasury duration and sovereign risk, so country-level funding conditions remained more informative than the aggregate move. A softer dollar aided hard-currency borrowers and commodity importers, but it did not settle refinancing risk for issuers with short maturities, weak reserves or dependence on foreign inflows.

Turkey and Colombia remained examples of that vulnerability because both rely more heavily on external capital and would face a sharper tightening if the Fed returned to a hawkish path. Oil’s large monthly decline helped importers’ trade and inflation arithmetic, while reduced energy revenue worked against exporters. July 2 eased the common dollar-liquidity pressure; it did not produce a uniform sovereign outcome. Local-currency debt, hard-currency bonds and corporate funding continued to respond to different combinations of policy credibility, reserve capacity and terms of trade.

## Cross asset

Cross assets recorded at the 5:00 p.m. ET New York reference mostly reinforced the front-end policy repricing after fixed income had already closed. The technology selloff was underway before 2:00 p.m., but final equity, volatility, currency and commodity values are later evidence. They can show whether the rates interpretation persisted through the rest of the day; they cannot reverse the causal order of the shortened Treasury session.

Equity performance exposed sharp internal dispersion. The S&P 500 ended essentially unchanged at 7,483.24, while the Nasdaq 100 fell 1.61% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index dropped 5.4448%. The VIX declined 2.6522% to 16.15 despite those losses. Broad stability and lower implied volatility argued against a systemic growth shock, while the chip rout challenged confidence in the sector carrying a large share of investment-grade borrowing and capital expenditure.

The dollar and gold aligned more directly with reduced near-term Fed pressure. The Dollar Index fell 0.5227% to 100.86, and gold rose 1.0914% to $4,112.70. A weaker dollar eased global funding conditions and matched the decline in immediate hike probability. Gold’s gain also reflected lower yields and renewed demand for a non-yielding asset, though it did not signal a generalized haven episode when broad equities were flat and volatility fell.

Energy preserved the disinflationary medium-term context without adding a fresh daily impulse. WTI settled at $68.69, up 0.1604%, and Brent at $71.80, up 0.3214%. Over 1 month they were lower by 25.4666% and 24.4051%. The near-flat daily closes explain why 10-year breakevens held steady after their recent retreat. The much larger monthly decline remained central to the reduction in inflation anxiety cited by Fed and ECB officials.

Taken together, later markets confirmed a narrower interpretation of the Treasury move. Dollar weakness and gold strength supported less urgent Fed tightening; flat broad equities and falling volatility denied the labor miss a recessionary reading. Semiconductor losses complicated the growth story and highlighted credit concentration, yet they did not generate a broad haven rally. The unresolved cross-asset question is whether the AI correction stays sector-specific or begins to weaken investment, issuance and risk appetite enough to pull long real yields lower.

## Forward Market Significance

The first post-holiday US macro sequence begins July 6 at 10:00 a.m. ET with ISM services and the New York Fed Global Supply Chain Pressure Index, followed July 7 by May trade at 8:30 a.m. and the June Survey of Consumer Expectations at 11:00. The June 16-17 FOMC minutes arrive July 8 at 2:00 p.m. A softer services-employment and prices mix, contained logistics costs and easing household inflation expectations would confirm that the payroll slowdown can keep July policy unchanged, with the strongest response in 2- to 5-year yields. Resilient services demand, renewed price pressure or minutes showing broad support for a 2026 increase would challenge that front-end relief and restore the restrictive reaction function that drove the prior week’s selloff.

Treasury absorption becomes a separate test from July 6 through July 9. The July 6 sales comprise $92 billion of 13-week bills and $79 billion of 26-week bills at 11:30 a.m. ET; July 7 brings $90 billion of 42-day bills, $52 billion of 52-week bills and a $58 billion 3-year note. Reopenings of $39 billion in 10-year notes on July 8 and $22 billion in 30-year bonds on July 9 complete the sequence. Broad bidding, contained dealer awards and orderly July 9 bill and July 15 coupon settlements would resolve the immediate holiday-funding question while supporting the current curve split. Weak participation or persistent secondary cheapening would reopen balance-sheet concerns and transmit from cash bills into front-end liquidity or from coupon supply into the long-end term premium.

The global-duration divergence faces its clearest test in Japan and the euro area. Japan’s roughly ¥3.8 trillion 3-month-bill auction is due July 3, its approximately ¥600 billion 30-year JGB sale July 7 and its roughly ¥2.5 trillion 5-year auction July 9; euro-area producer prices and retail sales arrive July 6 at 5:00 a.m. ET. Strong Japanese end-investor demand alongside softer European pipeline prices and consumption would challenge the persistence of July 2’s global term-premium restraint and allow US long yields to respond more fully to weaker labor. Another weak JGB auction, renewed yen pressure or firm euro-area prices would confirm policy and buyer-base dispersion, keeping the long end sensitive to real-yield and supply compensation even if the Fed remains on hold.

section_summaries.market_read: The close removed an immediate policy shock but left the long-end clearing hurdle intact.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether weaker growth evidence finally pulls long real yields lower after the holiday.
section_summaries.summary: July 2 reversed immediate hike anxiety through softer labor data and lower oil, producing a front-end-led steepening rather than a broad duration rally. Stable inflation compensation, elevated real yields, Japanese term-premium pressure and concentrated AI financing needs kept longer-maturity and credit risks unresolved.

section_summaries.market_read: Fed urgency eased at the front end, while real-yield demand preserved a steep-curve bias.
section_summaries.next_check: Services prices and employment will show whether front-end relief can extend beyond payrolls.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Payroll disappointment and weaker participation shifted Fed pricing toward a later move, driving a policy-led bull steepening without easing long-end real compensation. Contained layoffs, intact production pipelines and positive weekly yield changes kept the move measured and short of a regime shift.

section_summaries.market_read: Open funding channels coexist with a concentration premium that aggregate pricing understates.
section_summaries.next_check: Track secondary performance and dealer absorption as AI-linked issuance continues.
section_summaries.summary: Listed debt gauges later showed resilient broad risk tone, but tight high-grade pricing masks rising dependence on a narrow group of AI borrowers. Strong ratings and cash flows support access, while repeated long-dated supply and uncertain project returns reduce the margin for post-issuance disappointment.

section_summaries.market_read: Global easing signals no longer guarantee lower long yields when buyer demand and supply diverge.
section_summaries.next_check: Japan’s next auctions will test whether weak end demand keeps pressure on sovereign term premia.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Euro-area disinflation reduced near-term tightening urgency, but Japan’s weak auction demand, foreign selling, fiscal ambition and yen-linked inflation pressure raised the global duration hurdle. The result is policy dispersion: softer European conditions can coexist with a high clearing rate for US and Japanese long bonds.

section_summaries.market_read: Dollar relief improved EM liquidity at the margin, but domestic credibility still governs absorption.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether Fed relief persists without currency weakness forcing restrictive local policy.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A softer dollar and lower near-term Fed urgency eased the common financing hurdle for emerging markets, supporting currencies and later listed debt tone. Yet restrictive local policy, uneven energy exposure and reliance on foreign inflows preserved country-level dispersion and left vulnerable borrowers sensitive to a hawkish reversal.

section_summaries.market_read: Later cross assets confirmed policy relief but challenged the AI-led growth and funding story.
section_summaries.next_check: The key test is whether semiconductor weakness spreads into investment, issuance or broad risk.
section_summaries.summary: After fixed income closed, dollar weakness and gold strength supported the reduced-hike interpretation, while flat broad equities and lower volatility denied a systemic growth shock. The semiconductor rout instead concentrated concern around AI investment, issuance and the durability of long real-yield pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The curve needs both softer policy inputs and credible long-duration absorption to rally broadly.
section_summaries.next_check: Follow the July 6-9 data and auction sequence for a break in the front-end/long-end divide.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will decide whether front-end relief can broaden: the post-holiday US macro sequence, Treasury’s bill and coupon supply, and Japanese auctions alongside euro-area data. Softer activity and orderly bidding would validate the split; renewed prices pressure or weak duration demand would restore tightening and term-premium risk.

methodology_note: U.S. fixed-income rows are same-date official daily references for the SIFMA-recommended session ending at 2:00 p.m. ET; their source timestamps are preserved. Later-closing cross-asset rows appear only when same-date data is available.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.26% (+1 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.23% (0 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 35 bp (+4 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 84 bp (+4 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Soft payrolls lift front-end Treasuries, but real-yield pressure holds the long end
overall_summary.session_narrative: By the SIFMA-recommended 2:00 p.m. ET close, weaker payroll growth, downward revisions and lower participation had shifted Fed pricing toward a later move, pulling the front end lower. Stable breakevens, positive long real-yield changes and weak Japanese duration demand limited the rally beyond 5 years. Later dollar weakness, gold strength and resilient listed credit confirmed the narrower policy-relief reading, while the semiconductor selloff remained concentrated rather than systemic.
overall_summary.summary: Softer employment data reduced the urgency of a near-term Fed increase and supported policy-sensitive Treasuries. Longer maturities resisted as real yields, supply needs and global term-premium pressure remained firm, leaving the early-close curve steeper rather than delivering a broad duration rally.

watch_events.market_implication: Softness would extend relief in 2- to 5-year yields; resilience would restore hike risk.
watch_events.market_trigger: Soft employment and prices with easing expectations, or renewed demand and broad hike support.

watch_events.market_trigger: Broad demand and orderly settlement, or weak participation with persistent secondary cheapening.
watch_events.title: Treasury supply and settlement test
watch_events.watch_line: Watch bidding breadth, dealer awards and secondary performance through bill and coupon sales.

watch_events.market_implication: Improvement would free US duration to rally; renewed dispersion would keep long yields pressured.
